Description: Unique identifier assigned to each individual in the dataset.
Age of the individual in years.
Gender of the individual.
Number of panic attacks experienced during a specific period (e.g., per month).
Average duration of a panic attack in minutes.
Primary factor that triggers panic attacks (e.g., Stress, Social Situations, Trauma, Crowds, Health Concerns, Unknown).
Heart rate (beats per minute) recorded during a panic attack.
Indicates whether excessive sweating occurred during the panic attack.
Indicates whether the individual experienced difficulty breathing.
Indicates whether dizziness was experienced during the panic attack.
Indicates whether chest pain or chest discomfort occurred.
Indicates whether the individual experienced shaking or trembling.
Previous medical or mental health conditions (e.g., Anxiety Disorder, Depression, Hypertension, None).
Indicates whether the individual is taking medication for panic attacks or related conditions.
Average daily caffeine consumption (e.g., number of cups of coffee/tea or mg per day).
Number of exercise sessions per week.
Average number of hours slept per night.
Frequency or level of alcohol consumption.
Indicates whether the individual is a smoker.
Indicates whether the individual is currently receiving therapy or counseling.
Overall panic severity score calculated based on symptoms and clinical assessment. Higher values indicate more severe panic symptoms.
